attributes.description: Alerts when GPU framebuffer (VRAM) usage exceeds 90% (`DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED / total > 0.90`). Near-full VRAM risks OOM kills for ML workloads.
attributes.name: [NVIDIA GPU OTel] Framebuffer memory utilization high

investigation_guide.blob:
## NVIDIA GPU framebuffer memory utilization high

### What fired
Framebuffer usage exceeded 90% on at least one GPU during the window.

### Why it matters
GPU memory is the most common failure mode for ML and inference workloads. Above 90% utilisation, large allocations fail with `cudaErrorMemoryAllocation` and training jobs crash.

### Triage
1. Identify the GPU via Hostname / UUID / modelName.
2. Inspect the workload — recent batch-size or model-size changes.
3. Check for memory leaks in long-running processes.

### Remediation
- Reduce batch size or enable gradient checkpointing.
- Move workloads to a GPU with more VRAM.
- Restart leaking processes.

### Tuning
- 90% threshold. ML training routinely operates at 85-95%; tune to your workload tolerance.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-nvidia_gpu.otel-default
| WHERE DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED IS NOT NULL AND DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE IS NOT NULL
| EVAL total_fb = DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED + DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE
| EVAL fb_used_pct = ROUND(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED / total_fb * 100.0, 2)
// Alert when framebuffer usage exceeds 90%
// Adjust threshold based on workload — ML training may routinely use 85-95%
| WHERE fb_used_pct > 90
| STATS
    max_used_pct = MAX(fb_used_pct),
    avg_used_pct = AVG(fb_used_pct),
    min_free_mb = MIN(DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_FREE)
  BY Hostname, UUID, modelName
| SORT max_used_pct DESC
